principles: Use the same pipeline-plus-agent architecture as the Russian workflow.
Let deterministic rules remove obvious non-body material before any model call.
Preserve body-text meaning and order; never rewrite or summarize content.
Prefer archiving notes and bibliographies over silently mixing them into the body corpus.
Escalate structural ambiguity to model review and repair instead of guessing.

ingestion.goal: Extract page text first; OCR only when extraction quality is suspicious or the page is image-only.

rules.goal: Strip low-risk note markers, repeated headers/footers, line-wrap artifacts, and deterministic reference tails.

agent.goal: Restore headings, paragraph boundaries, and resolve ambiguous note-vs-body cases.

review.goal: Tag residual issues and route only risky pages into repair.

repair.goal: Fix remaining suffix references, heading merges, and parallel-text remnants.

final_outputs.body_text: Primary clean text for RAG ingestion.
final_outputs.notes_archive: Structured note/endnote text kept outside the body corpus.
final_outputs.bibliography_archive: References/works-cited text kept outside the body corpus.

must_clean.description: Remove discretionary soft hyphen characters inserted by OCR or PDF extraction.
Strip zero-width and other invisible formatting characters.
Rejoin English words broken by line-wrap hyphenation across lines.
Merge line breaks inside normal prose paragraphs when the next line is not a heading, list, or table row.
Remove repeated page headers, footers, and running page numbers.
Collapse duplicate spaces and normalize spacing around punctuation.
Delete low-risk inline note markers such as word12, [12], or superscript note numbers when clearly not body values.
Trim trailing reference-only or note-only suffix blocks from body pages using deterministic cues.

drop.description: Drop title pages, acknowledgements, publication logistics, marketing pages, and other non-body front matter.
Drop table-of-contents pages from the body corpus.
Drop back-of-book index pages from the body corpus.
Drop publisher logistics, printing details, sales/contact pages, and similar metadata.
Drop pages that are clearly mojibake, watermark-only, or unrecoverable extraction noise.

archive.description: Archive footnote/endnote pages or note-heavy suffix pages instead of leaving them in the body corpus.
Archive references/bibliography/works-cited pages separately from body text.

conditional_clean.description: Bracket citations and author-year citations may be removable from body text, but should be reviewed if they might be part of scholarly argument.
A page may contain English text paired with Russian translation or vice versa; route to review rather than merging blindly.
Legal article numbers, section numbers, and clause numbering must not be mistaken for note markers.
A body page may still contain a trailing bibliography or notes suffix after rule cleanup.

protected_keep.description: Preserve true lexical hyphens in English words and names.
Preserve Roman numerals and section headings.
Preserve article numbers, section numbering, and clause labels.
Preserve dates, percentages, currency values, and measurement units.
Preserve URLs, emails, and identifiers only when they belong to body content rather than publisher metadata or references.

must_do: Keep only body text in the main cleaned stream.
Allow notes and bibliography material to be archived separately.
Restore headings and paragraph structure conservatively.
must_not_do: Rewrite garbled pages into fluent prose
Merge parallel translations into a single paragraph

must_do: Detect residual note markers, bibliography suffixes, glossary/index material, and parallel-text remnants.
Escalate ambiguous legal numbering instead of deleting it.
Remove residual note and bibliography suffixes on risky pages.
Repair heading merges and paragraph flattening.
Keep archived note/bibliography material out of the body corpus.
